zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Willoughby於1280年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛時，他的父親Sir Richard Willoughby20歲，母親Alice de Beke heiress of Eresby39歲。他於1310年與Isabel Mortain結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1362年3月14日享年82歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲。

nameMeanings.name: Willoughby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Willoughby, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire, and Warwickshire. They are named from an Old English +wilig ‘willow’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.

variants.name: Wilby
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Wilby, in Suffolk, Norfolk, and Northamptonshire. The first placename is probably named from an Old English wilig ‘willow’ + Old English bēag ‘circle, ring’; the Norfolk placename derives from Old English wilig + bēag or Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’; the Northamptonshire placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Vili + bý.
Redmonds has demonstrated that in the parish of Hipperholme, and subsequently elsewhere in Yorkshire, Wilby occurs as a variant of Willoughby .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Willoughby was born in 1280, in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Richard Willoughby, was 20 and his mother, Alice de Beke heiress of Eresby, was 39. He married Isabel Mortain in 1310. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 14 March 1362, at the age of 82, and was buried in Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Willoughbyが1280年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアロウボロウで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Willoughbyは20歳，母Alice de Beke heiress of Eresbyは39歳であった。1310年にIsabel Mortainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1362年3月14日に82歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウィロウビー オン ス ウオルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Willoughby(이)가 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 루그h버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Willoughby(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Beke heiress of Eresby(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1310년 Isabel Mortain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1362년 3월 14일 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 윌루그h비  테 울드즈에 묻혔다.

ru.Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire: уиллоугби он т волдс, ноттингхамшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Willoughby родился в 1280 г. в местности лоугбороую, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Willoughby было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Beke heiress of Eresby, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Mortain в 1310 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 14 марта 1362 г. в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности уиллоугби он т волдс, ноттингхамшир, Англия.
